# Commitment Issues

A free developer tool that generates official-looking death certificates for abandoned GitHub repositories.

## What it does

- Paste any public GitHub repo URL to get a printable death certificate
- Analyzes commit history, archive status, and activity decay to assign a cause of death
- Shows last commit as "last words" and repo stats (stars, issues, language)
- Generates shareable certificate images and embeddable README badges
- User graveyard scanner: check any GitHub username to see all dead, struggling, and alive repos

## Key pages

- https://commitmentissues.dev — main tool, paste a repo URL to generate a certificate
- https://commitmentissues.dev/about — how it works, privacy, terms
- https://commitmentissues.dev/user/[username] — graveyard report for a GitHub user

## API

- GET /api/repo?url={github_repo_url} — returns repo stats and generated cause of death
- GET /api/user?username={github_username} — returns all repos for a user with dead/struggling/alive classification
- GET /api/badge?username={github_username} — returns an SVG badge for GitHub READMEs

## Notes

- Public repos only; no authentication required
- Data sourced from GitHub's public API
- Causes of death are algorithmic, not human-written
- Free to use; no account required
